title: vaccinium uliginosum subsp. gaultherioides
intro: Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. gaultherioides, also known as bog cranberry, is a sub-species of the bog cranberry plant, which is a type of low-growing, evergreen shrub native to acidic bogs in the Northern Hemisphere. The medicinal properties of Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. gaultherioides include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. gaultherioides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
